debug_info.goal:
S : Type
rule : RelInt S
n : ℕ
f : Fin n → List (RelInt S)
i : Fin n
j : Fin (f i).length
Hij : rule = (f i).get j
⊢ (∃ a, a ∈ Graphiti.Projects.Noc.fin_range n ∧ f a = f i) ∧ (f i)[↑j] ∈ f i

failed_attempts:
-- Prove that i is in fin_range n using properties of Fin and fin_range.
        simp only [fin_range, Fin.ext_iff]
        <;> induction n <;> simp_all [Fin.val_zero, Fin.ext_iff, List.member_cons, List.mem_replicate]
        <;> omega
      simp_all
      ⟩
  -- Now we know that f i is in the map, we can find the element (f i).get j in f i.
  have h₂ : (f i).get j ∈ f i := by
    -- The get operation retrieves an element from the list, so it is trivially in the list.
    have h₃ : (f i).get j ∈ f i := by
      apply List.get_mem
    exact h₃
  -- Combine the above results to show that (f i).get j is in the flattened list.
  exact ⟨f i, h₁, (f i).get j, h₂⟩

rfl
      rw [h₉] at *
      exact h₈
    have h₅ : rule = (f i)[↑j] := by
      simpa [Hij] using rfl
    rw [h₅]
    exact h₄
  exact h₃

<;> (try omega)
        exact h₅ i
      exact h₄
    -- Now we have i ∈ fin_range n, so f i is in the map
    simp_all [List.mem_map]
    <;>

    (try aesop)
  · -- Prove that (f i).get j is in f i
    have h₁ : j.val < (f i).length := j.is_lt
    have h₂ : (f i).get j ∈ f i := by
      apply List.get_mem
      <;> simp_all [Fin.is_lt]
      <;> omega
    exact h₂

<;>

  have h₄ : (f i).get j ∈ (fin_range n).map f |>.flatten := by
    -- Use List.mem_flatten.mpr to show that (f i).get j is in the flattened list.
    apply List.mem_flatten.mpr
    exact ⟨f i, h₂, h₃⟩
  -- Finally, use the fact that (f i)[j] is in the flattened list to conclude.
  exact h₄
